entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop affected by aflatoxin contamination and produced by smallholder farmers in Africa.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN CONTAMINATION
entities.description: The presence of aflatoxins in groundnuts, which poses health risks and impacts trade.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The region where aflatoxin contamination in groundnuts is a significant problem.

entities.name: PEANUT TRADE
entities.description: The commercial exchange of groundnuts, which is hindered by aflatoxin contamination.

entities.name: HEALTH RISK
entities.description: The danger to human health resulting from the consumption of aflatoxin-contaminated groundnuts.

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER FARMERS
entities.description: Farmers who produce groundnuts and require solutions to reduce aflatoxin contamination to benefit from production.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT VALUE CHAIN
entities.description: The series of stages from production to consumption of groundnuts where aflatoxin mitigation is necessary.

entities.name: HOLISTIC MITIGATION STRATEGIES
entities.description: Comprehensive approaches deployed during production and postharvest to reduce aflatoxin contamination.

entities.name: PRODUCTION
entities.description: The stage of the groundnut value chain where crops are grown.

entities.name: POSTHARVEST
entities.description: The stage of the groundnut value chain following the harvest of the crop.

entities.name: CONSUMER AWARENESS
entities.description: The level of knowledge among consumers regarding the risks of aflatoxin and safe consumption.

entities.name: SAFE END USE ALTERNATIVES
entities.description: Alternative ways to use groundnuts that ensure safety despite the risk of aflatoxin.
